class.name: TetraMesh4D
class.description: TetraMesh4D is the base class for all 4D tetrahedral meshes. Tetrahedral meshes are defined using vertices and tetrahedra, where each tetrahedron is defined by four vertices. Tetrahedral meshes are used to represent rendered 4D objects similar to how 3D objects are represented using triangles. To make a TetraMesh4D, use one of the descendant classes, such as [ArrayTetraMesh4D] or [BoxTetraMesh4D]. Advanced users can create custom tetrahedral mesh classes by extending TetraMesh4D.

method.name: _get_simplex_cell_boundary_normals
method.description: Callback method that should return the face normals of the tetrahedral cells. Do not call this method. This can be overridden by derived classes when creating a custom mesh type in GDScript or another scripting language. See [method get_simplex_cell_boundary_normals] for details of the returned data.

method.name: _get_simplex_cell_indices
method.description: Callback method that should return the cell indices of a tetrahedral mesh. Do not call this method. This can be overridden by derived classes when creating a custom mesh type in GDScript or another scripting language. See [method get_simplex_cell_indices] for details of the returned data.

method.name: _get_simplex_cell_texture_map
method.description: Callback method that should return the UVW mapping of the tetrahedral cells. Do not call this method. This can be overridden by derived classes when creating a custom mesh type in GDScript or another scripting language. See [method get_simplex_cell_texture_map] for details of the returned data.

method.name: _get_simplex_cell_vertex_normals
method.description: Callback method that should return the vertex normals of the tetrahedral cells. Do not call this method. This can be overridden by derived classes when creating a custom mesh type in GDScript or another scripting language. See [method get_simplex_cell_vertex_normals] for details of the returned data.

method.name: export_texture_map_mesh
method.description: Exports the tetrahedral mesh's UVW texture mapping as an [ArrayMesh]. This can help with visualizing the 3D texture space of the mesh, allowing you to create textures that can be applied to the mesh.

method.name: get_signed_distance_to_mesh
method.description: Returns the signed distance from a point in 4D space to the surface of the tetrahedral mesh. The distance is negative if the point is inside the mesh, positive if outside, and zero if on the surface. If the function fails to calculate a distance, it will return [constant @GDScript.INF]. [b]Note:[/b] The [method populate_inverse_metric_cache] function MUST be called at least once before calling this method.

method.name: get_simplex_cell_boundary_normals
method.description: Gets the face normals of the tetrahedral cells. Normals define which side of the cell is the front side, and therefore should be drawn. Face normals can also be used to calculate vertex normals to determine how lighting angles are calculated for the cell. Each Vector4 in this array is for one cell, and should be normalized. This array should either be one-fourth as long as the [method get_simplex_cell_indices] array, or empty for no normals, or else the mesh is invalid.

method.name: get_simplex_cell_indices
method.description: Gets the cell indices of the tetrahedral mesh. Each set of 4 integers represents the indices of the vertices that make up a tetrahedron. The vertices can be obtained using [method Mesh4D.get_vertices], or using the [method get_simplex_cell_positions] method. Integers in this array should not exceed the length of the vertices array.

method.name: get_simplex_cell_positions
method.description: Gets the positions of the tetrahedral cells. Each Vector4 in the array is a position in meters in 4D space. Tetrahedra are defined by four vertices, and the positions in this array are the positions of the vertices that make up the tetrahedra. This value is usually calculated from a combination of the cell indices and the vertices arrays, see [method get_simplex_cell_indices] and [method Mesh4D.get_vertices].

method.name: get_simplex_cell_texture_map
method.description: Gets the UVW mapping of the tetrahedral cells. Each Vector3 in this array is the texture space mapping for the vertex in the [method Mesh4D.get_vertices] array with the same index. This array should either be the same length as the [method Mesh4D.get_vertices] array, or empty for no UVW mapping, or else the mesh is invalid.

method.name: get_simplex_cell_vertex_normals
method.description: Gets the vertex normals of the tetrahedral cells. Vertex normals define how lighting angles are calculated for the cell. Each Vector4 in this array is for one cell, and should be normalized. This array should either be the same length as the [method get_simplex_cell_indices] array, or empty for no normals, or else the mesh is invalid.

method.name: populate_inverse_metric_cache
method.description: Populates the cache used for nearest-point calculations, such as in [method get_signed_distance_to_mesh]. This is used to speed up nearest-point calculations, to avoid having to recalculate the same data each time a nearest-point calculation is done. This needs to be rerun whenever the geometry of the mesh changes, such as when vertices or cells are modified, if future nearest-point calculations are expected.

method.name: raycast_intersects
method.description: Performs a raycast against the tetrahedral cells of the mesh. The [param local_from] parameter is the starting point of the ray relative to the mesh, and the [param local_direction] parameter is the direction of the ray relative to the mesh. The returned [Dictionary] may contain the following keys: - [code]"hit"[/code]: A boolean indicating whether the ray hit any part of the mesh. - [code]"distance"[/code]: The distance from the starting point to the hit point. - [code]"normal"[/code]: The normal at the hit point, perpendicular to the surface. - [code]"cell_index"[/code]: The index of the cell that was hit. If the ray does not hit any part of the mesh, the returned dictionary will be [code]{"hit": false}[/code].

method.name: raycast_intersects_fast
method.description: Performs a raycast against the tetrahedral cells of the mesh. The [param local_from] parameter is the starting point of the ray relative to the mesh, and the [param local_direction] parameter is the direction of the ray relative to the mesh. Returns immediately with [code]true[/code] if the ray intersects any part of the mesh, or [code]false[/code] if it does not. This is a faster function than [method raycast_simplex_cells], which will keep going after the first hit, returning more information about the intersection, while this function will stop after any intersection is found.

method.name: tetra_mesh_clear_cache
method.description: TetraMesh4D caches the edge data for performance reasons. This method clears the cache, forcing the tetrahedral mesh to recalculate the edge data the next time it is needed. You should run this method if you are making your own [TetraMesh4D]-derived class and you change the vertices or cells. You do not need to run this when using the built-in classes such as [ArrayTetraMesh4D] or [BoxTetraMesh4D], they will automatically clear the cache when needed.

method.name: to_array_tetra_mesh
method.description: Converts this TetraMesh4D to an [ArrayTetraMesh4D]. Even if the mesh is already an [ArrayTetraMesh4D], this method will make a copy, but this method is intended for converting subtypes such as [BoxTetraMesh4D]. The array mesh will have the same vertices, cells, and material as this mesh. The material will be shared between the two meshes, but everything else will be a copy. Converting a non-array mesh to an array mesh is useful for modifying the geometry of the mesh, such as adding or removing vertices or cells.

method.name: to_tetra_mesh
method.description: Converts this TetraMesh4D to a new TetraMesh4D that is not a [PolyMesh4D]. This method will use the best conversion available. For example, a [BoxPolyMesh4D] will be converted to a [BoxTetraMesh4D]. If no specialized conversion is available, the method will return an [ArrayTetraMesh4D]. Even if the mesh is already a non-poly TetraMesh4D, this method will make a copy, but this method is intended for converting subtypes such as [BoxTetraMesh4D], [OrthoplexTetraMesh4D], or [PolyMesh4D]-derived classes. The new mesh will have the same vertices, cells, and material as this mesh. The material will be shared between the two meshes, but everything else will be a copy.
